agent-dispatch.name: agent-dispatch
agent-dispatch.description: Dispatch a soul-hub agent by slug. Tier-1 capability per ADR-023 — the agentic primitive that collapses the legacy `agent:` step type into the component catalog. Runs the full dispatchAgent() loop including PTY+/goal convergence, artifact marker extraction, and per-step budget overrides.
agent-dispatch.when_to_use: A step needs a soul-hub agent's skills, persona, or tools — anything that requires the iterative `/goal` convergence loop, multi-tool workflow, or a per-agent system prompt. The PTY backend is itself an interactive claude session (stateful across its own /goal turns); cross-dispatch resume is the ADR-028 Phase 2 spike. Pick this when you'd otherwise have invoked `claude` with a custom agent definition.
agent-dispatch.when_not_to_use: A single-purpose LLM pass (use `inline-llm-pass` — cheaper, no agent setup; it now supports session/resume for sequential chains per ADR-028). A shell command (use `shell-exec`). A typed external system has a Tier-2 adapter already (prefer the adapter — e.g. `katib-build`, `vault-write`).

inputs.name: agent
inputs.description: Slug of the agent to run. Must match a file at `~/.claude/agents/<slug>.md` or `~/.soul-hub/data/agents/<slug>.yaml`.

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Prompt passed to the agent. Interpolation of `{{steps.X.outputs.Y}}` template variables must be resolved by the runner before passing here.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Extra context injected into the dispatch options. Capped at 4000 chars internally (ADR-005 D6 PTY paste-stall guard). Longer strings are silently truncated.

inputs.name: goal_condition
inputs.description: Natural-language convergence condition forwarded to the PTY dispatcher as `/goal <condition>`. When present, the agent self-iterates until the condition is met or the budget fires. Only the `claude-pty` backend acts on this; `claude-cli-flag` and `ai-sdk` ignore it.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Dispatch backend selection. `production` routes through claude-pty with `/goal` loop. `test` uses claude-cli-flag with hard caps ($0.10 / 5 turns / 60s) — for CI smokes. `oneshot` uses claude-cli-flag with no caps, for structurally single-pass agents that need production budgets.

inputs.name: timeout_sec
inputs.description: Wall-clock budget in seconds. Absent = agent's stored budget, which falls through to PRODUCTION_DEFAULTS (600s) if unset.

inputs.name: max_turns
inputs.description: Turn budget. Absent = agent's stored budget (default 25).

inputs.name: max_usd
inputs.description: Dollar ceiling. Absent = agent's stored budget (default $0.50).

outputs.name: output_excerpt
outputs.description: Last 2000 characters of the agent's final output.

outputs.name: artifact_path
outputs.description: Present when the agent emits the `===ARTIFACT===\n<path>\n===END===` marker in its output. Vault-relative path to the produced artefact.

outputs.name: agent_status
outputs.description: Terminal status from DispatchResult — one of `success`, `goal_achieved`, `failed`, `cancelled`, `timeout`, `error`, `budget-exceeded`.

outputs.name: num_turns
outputs.description: Number of turns the agent took.

outputs.name: cost_usd
outputs.description: Actual dollar cost of the dispatch run.

outputs.name: exit_code
outputs.description: 0 on success or goal_achieved. 1 on failed, timeout, cancelled, or error.

inputs.task:
Verify that the file projects/naseej/index.md exists in the current
working directory (which is the vault root). Use the Read tool once.
Report a single line — either exactly "VERIFIED: naseej index exists"
or exactly "MISSING: naseej index not found". Do not iterate, do not
run tests, do not invoke any other tools, do not write any files,
do not load any vault context. This is a minimum-spend dispatch smoke,
not a real QA pass.
inputs.goal_condition: a verification line was written to stdout

approval-gate.name: approval-gate
approval-gate.description: Pause a recipe run and require the operator to approve or reject before continuing. Uses the stdout-code-2 pause protocol — first invocation emits a pause-request and exits 2; the runner intercepts, registers the pause, awaits the operator's decision, then re-invokes the component with resume_response injected; second invocation exits 0 with decision + optional comment.
approval-gate.when_to_use: A binary continue-or-abort decision before a consequential step (sending a draft, deploying, destructive ops). Operator should be in the loop with no structured data to enter — only the buttons.
approval-gate.when_not_to_use: You need structured or free-form data beyond a yes/no (use `human-form`). The pipeline must run unattended — gate steps block forever without an operator. You only want a quality check, not a human approval (use `stop-slop` or `inline-llm-pass`).

inputs.prompt: CP4 smoke — approve the ADR-023 component-first migration?

callout.name: callout
callout.description: Boxed message with an accent border and tinted background. Tones map to conventional UX patterns — info/warn/danger/tip for status messages; neutral for non-status highlights (subject lines, non-binding notices).

inputs.name: tone
inputs.description: info | warn | danger | tip | neutral. neutral uses tag_bg + accent, for non-status highlights.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Optional bolded first line.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: The message body — plain text or minimal inline HTML.

sample_inputs.title: Summary
sample_inputs.body: A short highlighted note that draws the reader's attention to a key point.

categorical-heatmap.name: categorical-heatmap
categorical-heatmap.description: Generic row × column relevance heatmap, rendered as an HTML table of color-coded HIGH/MED/LOW cells. Columns and rows are inputs — no domain assumption.

inputs.name: columns
inputs.description: Column headers. The caller supplies them — no domain default.

inputs.name: rows
inputs.description: Rows to plot. Each is {label, cells: [high|med|low, ...]} aligned to columns.

sample_inputs.caption: Relevance of each row to each column (high / med / low).

channel-send-text.name: channel-send-text
channel-send-text.description: Send a text message via a channel adapter (telegram | whatsapp). Routes through POST /api/channels/send — the soul-hub adapter registry.
channel-send-text.when_to_use: Notify a person via Telegram or WhatsApp from a recipe — completion alert, ping, link to an artefact. Plain text or markdown (per adapter's parseMode).
channel-send-text.when_not_to_use: You need to send media (images, audio, files — no media support yet). You need a structured reply back from the user (use `human-form` or a gate component, not a channel). You're writing inside soul-hub app code with adapter access — call adapter.send directly.

inputs.name: channel
inputs.description: Channel adapter id. Must be enabled + configured in soul-hub settings.json.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: The message body. Plain text or markdown (per adapter's configured parseMode in settings.json). Long messages are chunked by the adapter.

inputs.name: api_base
inputs.description: Override the Soul Hub base URL.

outputs.name: message_id
outputs.description: Adapter-assigned message id (Telegram message_id, WhatsApp serialized id). Empty string when adapter doesn't return one.

outputs.name: delivered_at
outputs.description: ISO timestamp captured on the soul-hub side after adapter.send resolved.

outputs.name: ok
outputs.description: Always true on exit 0 — kept for symmetry with other components and forward-compat with multi-recipient sends.

comparison-dot-matrix.name: comparison-dot-matrix
comparison-dot-matrix.description: Generic two-column dot-grid matrix comparing two intensity scores across rows. Each row shows filled dots per column up to its score; below-score dots are outlined. Column + row-header labels are inputs. Domain-neutral.

inputs.name: row_header_label
inputs.description: Header for the row-label column. Defaults to 'Item'.

inputs.name: rows
inputs.description: Rows to plot. Each is {label, a, b} with a/b integers 0..max_intensity.

cover-page.name: cover-page
cover-page.description: Full-page document opener. Defaults to a minimalist-typographic layout (CSS-only); supply an `image` path and it full-bleeds that photo with a dark gradient overlay for legible white text. Optional logo + author from the brand layer; eyebrow, title, subtitle, and a footer reference code from inputs.

inputs.name: eyebrow
inputs.description: Category label above the title (e.g. Tutorial, Strategy brief). Usually a standing series label — set once.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The cover title.

inputs.name: subtitle
inputs.description: Supporting line under the title.

inputs.name: reference_code
inputs.description: Monospace code rendered in the footer strip. Usually computed (date / sequence) each run.

inputs.name: image
inputs.description: Absolute path (or file:// URI) to a full-bleed background photo. When present the cover switches to the image-background layout: the photo covers the page, a dark gradient overlay keeps the white title legible. Omit for the minimalist-typographic layout. Typically a deterministic slot — a pipeline picks today's image from a pool by date, no model call.

sample_inputs.title: Document Title
sample_inputs.subtitle: A supporting subtitle line that sets context for the document.

doc-render.name: doc-render
doc-render.description: Render an ordered composition of kind:presentation components to a single PDF via WeasyPrint. Naseej-owned document engine; replaces the external katib-build dependency.
doc-render.when_to_use: Produce a branded PDF from a sequence of presentation components (cover, module, callout, ...). Pass the ordered composition + a brand token map + lang; get back a rendered PDF path. The render engine for any document recipe.
doc-render.when_not_to_use: Score or rewrite prose (use stop-slop or inline-llm-pass). Run a non-presentation subprocess primitive (this only composes kind:presentation catalog entries). Generate charts or fetch remote images — the image/cover provider layer is Phase 2, not in this engine yet.

inputs.name: composition
inputs.description: Ordered list of presentation-component instances. Each entry is an object with `component` (a kind:presentation slug in the catalog) and `inputs` (the data slots that component's template fills). Rendered top to bottom into one document. Required unless `composition_path` is supplied.

inputs.name: composition_path
inputs.description: Path to a JSON file holding the composition — either a bare array or an object with a `composition` key. Use when the composition is too large for the caller's inline value limit (e.g. a recipe step's 10KB stdout cap): the producer writes it to a file and passes the path. Ignored when `composition` is supplied inline.

inputs.name: brand
inputs.description: Brand layer merged over the shipped base tokens. Three forms: an inline token map (colors/fonts/identity/logo); a string path to a brand YAML file (has / or .yaml/.yml); or a bare brand SLUG resolved against the catalog brands dir (ADR-031). Omit for the neutral defaults.

inputs.name: brands_dir
inputs.description: Override the dir a brand SLUG resolves against. Defaults to the `brands` sibling of catalog_root (catalog/brands). Used by tests.

inputs.name: lang
inputs.description: Render language. Picks template.ar when present (else falls back to the en template under dir=rtl, with a warning) and the ar font stack + per-language identity.

inputs.name: out_pdf
inputs.description: Output path for the rendered PDF. Parent dirs are created. Returned, resolved, as `pdf_path`.

inputs.name: catalog_root
inputs.description: Override the component search dir. Defaults to this component's parent (catalog/components/). Used by tests to point at a fixture catalog.

outputs.name: pdf_path
outputs.description: Absolute path to the rendered PDF.

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Size of the rendered PDF in bytes.

outputs.name: components_rendered
outputs.description: Count of composition entries rendered into the document.

outputs.name: warnings
outputs.description: Non-fatal notes (e.g. an ar render that fell back to the en template, or a declared styles file that was missing). Empty on a clean render.

horizontal-bar-chart.name: horizontal-bar-chart
horizontal-bar-chart.description: Generic horizontal bar chart drawn as inline SVG. Bars auto-scale to the largest value and are color-coded by intensity tier (high / mid / low). Value labels render next to each bar. Domain-neutral: framing comes from inputs.

inputs.name: items
inputs.description: Bars to plot. Each is {label, value, value_display, intensity: high|mid|low}.

sample_inputs.caption: Values by category; bars scale to the largest.

human-form.name: human-form
human-form.description: Pause a recipe run and collect structured operator input via a prompt + optional field schema. Uses the stdout-code-2 pause protocol — first invocation emits a pause-request and exits 2; the runner intercepts, registers the pause, awaits the operator's response, then re-invokes the component with resume_response injected; second invocation exits 0 with the operator's payload.
human-form.when_to_use: Collect free-form or structured data from the operator mid-recipe — confirmation with comment, manual data entry for a downstream step, schema-driven form responses. Use the `fields[]` input for typed fields.
human-form.when_not_to_use: A binary approved/rejected decision is enough (use `approval-gate` — simpler UI). The pipeline must run unattended (gates block forever without an operator). You can derive the value without human input (compute it with `inline-llm-pass` or `shell-exec`).

inputs.prompt: CP4 smoke — what is your favourite Naseej component slug?

inline-llm-pass.name: inline-llm-pass
inline-llm-pass.description: Run a single-purpose LLM pass via `claude -p --output-format=json`. Tier-1 capability per ADR-006 D4 — generic prompt-in/text-out wrapper for recipes that need a one-shot model call without the iterative `/goal` loop or an agent definition. The decomposition primitive for any pipeline that splits work into narrow, parallelizable model calls.
inline-llm-pass.when_to_use: A single-pass model call — em-dash strip, polish pass, JSON extraction, decomposition step. Cheaper + faster than `agent-dispatch` because there is no agent loop. Use a narrow `system_prompt` to constrain.
inline-llm-pass.when_not_to_use: Needs multi-tool iteration / convergence (use `agent-dispatch`). Output is deterministic / rule-based (use `shell-exec` running a script — no model cost). Needs operator approval after generation (chain with `approval-gate` or `human-form`).

inputs.name: prompt
inputs.description: The full prompt sent to the model. Plain text — no system/user role markers. If you need to operate on existing text, pass it via `input_text` and reference it from the prompt body.

inputs.name: input_text
inputs.description:
Optional content the prompt should operate on. Appended to the prompt under a `

---

# Input

<text>` block before invocation. Useful for decomposed pipelines that pass the previous step's output forward. Mutually exclusive with `input_text_path`.

inputs.name: input_text_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to a file whose contents become `input_text`. Use this when the upstream step's output exceeds shell-exec's 10 KB inline cap — pair with the upstream's `stdout_to_file` and pass its `outputs.stdout_path` here. Mutually exclusive with `input_text`.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: Model slug passed verbatim as `--model`. Defaults to Sonnet 4.6 — Opus 4.7 is overkill for single-purpose polish passes, Haiku 4.5 may not survive stop-slop work. Recipe authors override per-step for cost/quality tuning.

inputs.name: system_prompt
inputs.description: Optional system-level framing passed as `--append-system-prompt`. Use for narrow remit ("You are an em-dash stripping editor. Reply with the rewritten text only — no preamble."). Reduces token count vs stuffing the framing into `prompt`.

inputs.name: budget_seconds
inputs.description: Wall-clock cap before SIGTERM (5s SIGKILL grace). 120s is generous for single-pass work; tune down to 60s for narrow polish passes, up to 300s for long drafts.

inputs.name: claude_binary
inputs.description: Absolute path to the `claude` executable. Tests override to point at a stub script. Production leaves unset — resolves `claude` from PATH.

inputs.name: cwd
inputs.description: Working directory for the spawned claude process. Defaults to `/tmp` (a clean dir) on purpose — running from a directory with a CLAUDE.md auto-loads ~10× the per-call token cost. Override only when the call genuinely needs project context (rare for decomposed pipelines).

inputs.name: max_budget_usd
inputs.description: Optional per-call dollar ceiling (passed as `--max-budget-usd`). Bounds runaway risk on long generation. Recommend setting on every call in production recipes — even $0.10 catches infinite-loop bugs that would otherwise burn $5 before the wall-clock budget fires.

inputs.name: json_schema
inputs.description: Optional JSON Schema (passed as `--json-schema`). When set, the model's output MUST conform to the schema or the CLI returns is_error=true. Eliminates an entire class of malformed-output failures for structured generation (findings, tables, metadata). Leave unset for prose generation where structure is loose.

inputs.name: session_action
inputs.description: ADR-028 session mode. `start` opens a persistent claude conversation (drops --no-session-persistence, passes --session-id) and returns its `session_id`. `continue` resumes a prior session (--resume) so this turn has the full context of earlier turns — enables cross-step consistency and stop-slop-as-remediation. Omit for the default stateless one-shot call. Session mode is sequential — a session cannot be resumed from parallel steps.

inputs.name: session_id
inputs.description: UUID of the session to use. With `session_action=continue` this is REQUIRED and names the session to resume. With `session_action=start` it is optional — when omitted the component generates a UUID and returns it. Thread it forward via `{{steps.<start-step>.outputs.session_id}}`. Ignored when `session_action` is unset. A session chain MUST share the same `cwd` across steps (claude keys sessions by project dir).

outputs.name: text
outputs.description: The model's response text, extracted from the CLI envelope's `result` field. Trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace.

outputs.name: exit_code
outputs.description: 0 ok / 2 bad input / 124 timeout / 1 CLI error (envelope `is_error=true` or non-zero CLI exit). Mirrors the wrapped CLI's exit so the runner's halt-on-non-zero fires correctly.

outputs.name: duration_ms
outputs.description: Wall-clock time the subprocess ran for.

outputs.name: cost_usd
outputs.description: Cost from the CLI envelope's `total_cost_usd`. May be 0 for cached or zero-cost branches.

outputs.name: num_turns
outputs.description: Turn count from the CLI envelope. Always 1 for inline-llm-pass (no /goal loop) — surfaced for symmetry with agent-step metrics.

outputs.name: model_used
outputs.description: Model slug actually used by the CLI (may differ from `model` input if the CLI falls back).

outputs.name: timed_out
outputs.description: True when budget_seconds was exceeded and SIGTERM was sent. exit_code is then 124.

outputs.name: session_id
outputs.description: Present only in session mode (`session_action` set). The session UUID — for `start` the opened/generated id, for `continue` the resumed id. Thread to the next step's `session_id` input to chain the conversation.

inputs.system_prompt:
You are a peer-brief author for an executive readership in the GCC.
Voice: warm + professional + visionary; analytical, not consultative.
We build the brief over several turns in this conversation. Hold the
context I give you now in mind for every later turn.

STOP-SLOP DISCIPLINE (apply to every turn):
- NO em-dashes (the long dash or a double hyphen). Use commas, periods, or rephrase.
- Vary sentence rhythm. Avoid three consecutive clauses of similar length.
- Cite signal counts and source names from the context.
- Plain English. Avoid corporate slop (leverage, unlock, robust, delve).

OUTPUT FORMAT (this turn): reply with ONE valid JSON object and nothing
else. No code fence, no preamble. Keys exactly:
  {
    "cover_title": "<2-5 word title — see COVER CRAFT below>",
    "cover_subtitle": "<75-110 word single cohesive paragraph — see COVER CRAFT below. End with: By Jasem Al Neaimi, jneaimi.com>",
    "exec_summary": "<200-300 word summary: signal count, number of findings, today's hinge claim, which pillar dominates, a 1-2 sentence operator framing>"
  }
All values are plain prose strings (no HTML in this turn). Remember the
title and hinge you choose; later turns must stay consistent with them.

COVER CRAFT (the title + subtitle are the most-read 20 words of the whole
brief — they are the editorial centerpiece, not a label. Spend effort here):
- cover_title: 2 to 5 words. Imperative verb phrase OR a sharp, concrete
  noun phrase. It NAMES today's hinge, it does not describe it. No buzzwords,
  no "AI", no "this week".
    GOOD: "Control the Agent Stack" · "The App Store Moment"
    WEAK: "AI Trends This Week" · "Important Developments in Agents"
- cover_subtitle: exactly ONE cohesive paragraph, 75 to 110 words, that reads
  as a single connected argument — NOT a list of short declaratives. Arc:
  (1) open on the concrete pattern with a NUMBER or named source from the
  context, (2) turn to the tension or what is at stake, (3) land a forward
  claim for the GCC operator. Use connective tissue between sentences and
  vary their length. NEVER write three short staccato sentences in a row.
  Then end with exactly: By Jasem Al Neaimi, jneaimi.com
    GOOD shape (study the flow, not the content): "Five companies that build
    no models at all are quietly capturing the infrastructure that will govern
    every AI agent deployed at scale. Model capability is no longer the
    differentiating variable; runtime control, identity, and payment authority
    are. For GCC governments advancing under Vision 2030, these dimensions map
    onto data-residency law and procurement boundaries, and whoever defines
    them now sets the terms for regional AI governance for a decade."
    BAD shape (choppy + undershot — do NOT do this): "Vibe coding crossed into
    the App Store this week. The production gap remains. Governance is the
    question underneath both."
inputs.prompt: Produce the JSON object (cover_title, cover_subtitle, exec_summary) from the context below.
inputs.input_text:
===CONTEXT (parse JSON; summary_stats for counts, topics[0..4] for findings, breakthrough for the hinge, content_opportunities for the pillar distribution)===
{{steps.build-context.outputs.stdout}}

katib-build.name: katib-build
katib-build.description: DEPRECATED (ADR-029/030) — renders a recipe to PDF via the EXTERNAL ~/dev/katib CLI. Superseded by the internal `doc-render` engine, which composes kind:presentation components with zero katib dependency. Retained only for the live `peer-brief-daily-naseej-v2` shadow until ADR-034 CP3 cutover, then removed. Do NOT use in new recipes.
katib-build.when_to_use: Do not use in new recipes — use `doc-render` (the internal engine). This adapter exists only until the last katib-dependent shadow is retired at ADR-034 CP3.
katib-build.when_not_to_use: Any new document render — use `doc-render` with kind:presentation components (no external katib dependency, brand-by-slug, slot-aware).

inputs.name: recipe_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to the katib recipe YAML (the synthesised recipe, not the Naseej recipe). Passed verbatim as the first positional arg to scripts/build.py.

inputs.name: out_pdf
inputs.description: Absolute path where katib should write the rendered PDF.

inputs.name: lang
inputs.description: Render language — en (LTR) or ar (RTL). Passed as `--lang`.

inputs.name: brand
inputs.description: Brand identifier resolved by katib's brand registry. Passed as `--brand`.

inputs.name: skip_audit_check
inputs.description: When true, passes `--skip-audit-check` to katib (the daily peer-brief path; PDFs that are not audit-gated). Set false for audit-gated builds.

inputs.name: katib_project_dir
inputs.description: Katib repo dir. Defaults to ~/dev/katib. Override for tests or sibling katib clones.

inputs.name: timeout_sec
inputs.description: Wall-clock cap on the katib subprocess. Typical daily peer-brief builds complete in 30-60s; default 300 gives headroom for cold WeasyPrint imports + large signal-cluster pages.

outputs.name: pdf_path
outputs.description: Absolute path of the rendered PDF (echo of out_pdf input on success).

outputs.name: pdf_size_bytes
outputs.description: Size of the rendered PDF on disk. Useful for parity checks during shadow runs.

outputs.name: build_duration_ms
outputs.description: Wall-clock time the katib subprocess ran.

outputs.name: brand_resolved
outputs.description: Echo of the brand input — surfaced so the recipe can branch on it without re-templating.

outputs.name: error_summary
outputs.description: Set only on failure. Last ERROR/WeasyPrint line found in the build.log tail (last 40 lines). For full triage, read build_log_tail.

outputs.name: build_log_tail
outputs.description: Set only on failure. Last 20 lines of combined stdout+stderr from katib.

module.name: module
module.description: Flexible body unit — optional numbered eyebrow, heading, intro, and body. All heading fields are optional; use without a title for continuous prose. `body` is auto-escaped plain text; `raw_body` accepts trusted HTML (tables, pull-quotes, nested callouts).

inputs.name: number
inputs.description: Module number rendered as a large accent-colored digit (numbered variant only).

inputs.name: eyebrow
inputs.description: Above-title category label (e.g. Module 1 · Foundations).

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Module heading. Omit for continuous prose sections (letter bodies, abstracts).

inputs.name: intro
inputs.description: Paragraph under the title setting context for the body.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Plain-text body. Auto-escaped — safe for any source.

inputs.name: raw_body
inputs.description: Trusted HTML body. NOT auto-escaped — recipe-authored content only. Takes precedence over body.

sample_inputs.title: Section heading
sample_inputs.intro: A short paragraph that sets context for what follows.
sample_inputs.body: Body text for the section — plain prose that flows under the heading.

pull-quote.name: pull-quote
pull-quote.description: Emphasized blockquote — larger italic text with an accent rule (rule-leading) or a big opening quote mark (large-quote), plus optional attribution.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: The quote body.

inputs.name: attribution
inputs.description: Attribution line (author, source, role).

sample_inputs.text: A concise, emphasized statement that deserves visual weight on the page.

shell-exec.name: shell-exec
shell-exec.description: Run an arbitrary subprocess with stdin/stdout/stderr capture, optional file redirection, and a wall-clock timeout. Tier-1 capability per ADR-006 D4 — the generic escape hatch for invoking external commands from a recipe before the catalog accretes a typed Tier-2 adapter.
shell-exec.when_to_use: Invoke an external command from a recipe — script, CLI tool, binary. No Tier-2 adapter exists yet for that system. Pair with `stdout_to_file` when the upstream output exceeds 10 KB inline.
shell-exec.when_not_to_use: A Tier-2 adapter exists for the system (prefer it — typed inputs, error parsing): `katib-build`, `vault-write`, `channel-send-text`. You need shell features like `&&`, `|`, globs — pass a script path instead of a chained command, or invoke `bash -c` deliberately. The command is a one-shot LLM call (use `inline-llm-pass`).

inputs.name: cmd
inputs.description: Executable name or absolute path. Resolved via PATH (no shell, so no `&&`, `|`, or globs — pass args separately).

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: Argument vector passed verbatim to the executable. Each element is a single argv slot — no shell-word splitting.

inputs.name: cwd
inputs.description: Working directory for the subprocess. Defaults to soul-hub repo root (the runner's process.cwd).

inputs.name: stdin
inputs.description: Text piped to the subprocess's stdin (closed immediately after). Empty by default.

inputs.name: env
inputs.description: Environment variables merged onto process.env. Use sparingly — secrets should come from the operator shell, not from recipes.

inputs.name: timeout_sec
inputs.description: Wall-clock cap before the runner sends SIGTERM (then SIGKILL after a 5s grace). Tune up for long renders (katib build, signal-trend extract); tune down for cheap checks.

inputs.name: stdout_to_file
inputs.description: Absolute path. When set, the subprocess's stdout is captured to this file in addition to being truncated inline in `outputs.stdout`. Pair with `{{work_dir}}/<name>` for run-scoped artefacts.

inputs.name: stderr_to_file
inputs.description: Same as stdout_to_file but for stderr.

outputs.name: exit_code
outputs.description: Subprocess exit code (or -1 if spawn failed, 124 on timeout per GNU convention).

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.description: Captured stdout, truncated at 10KB inline. Full payload at stdout_path if stdout_to_file was set.

outputs.name: stderr
outputs.description: Captured stderr, truncated at 10KB inline. Full payload at stderr_path if stderr_to_file was set.

outputs.name: stdout_path
outputs.description: Absolute path to the captured stdout file. Present only when stdout_to_file was supplied.

outputs.name: stderr_path
outputs.description: Absolute path to the captured stderr file. Present only when stderr_to_file was supplied.

outputs.name: duration_ms
outputs.description: Wall-clock time the subprocess ran for.

outputs.name: timed_out
outputs.description: True when the runner sent SIGTERM due to timeout_sec being exceeded. exit_code is then 124.

stacked-area-chart.name: stacked-area-chart
stacked-area-chart.description: Generic stacked-area chart drawn as inline SVG from pre-computed polygon bands over an x-axis, with optional ticks, legend, and a highlighted column. Domain-neutral: all framing comes from inputs.

inputs.name: bands
inputs.description: Stacked area bands, bottom to top. Each is {cluster, color, points, today_value}.

inputs.name: x_ticks
inputs.description: X-axis ticks. Each is {x, label}.

inputs.name: y_ticks
inputs.description: Y-axis ticks. Each is {y, label}.

inputs.name: today_x
inputs.description: X coordinate for the highlighted column rule.

inputs.name: chart_geometry
inputs.description: SVG geometry. {view_w, view_h, chart_top, chart_bottom, chart_left, chart_right}.

sample_inputs.title: Volume over time
sample_inputs.caption: Stacked series volume over the window; latest column highlighted.

stop-slop.name: stop-slop
stop-slop.description: Content quality gate — deterministic anti-slop scanner with 0-50 score and HARD-violation blocking
stop-slop.when_to_use: Score prose against the anti-slop rubric before publish/send. Block (exit 6) on score below threshold OR any hard violation. Cheap, fast, deterministic — no model cost.
stop-slop.when_not_to_use: Need qualitative rewriting / model-driven polish (use `inline-llm-pass` with a stop-slop framing prompt). Need a human approval rather than a rubric (use `approval-gate`). Need to score a non-English language other than the rubric covers — only `default` is implemented in v1.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: The prose to score. UTF-8. Newlines preserved. HTML tags stripped before scanning.

inputs.name: rubric
inputs.description: Banned-phrase set to apply. v1.0.0 only implements `default`; other values accepted as forward-compat but route to `default`.

inputs.name: min_score
inputs.description: Minimum total score (0-50). Below this, `passed` is false.

inputs.name: block_on_fail
inputs.description: When true, non-zero exit code (6) on fail. When false, always exit 0 and let the caller decide.

outputs.name: score
outputs.description: Total 0-50. Sum of 5 dimensions (each 0-10, floored).

outputs.name: passed
outputs.description: True iff score >= min_score AND hard_violation_count == 0.

outputs.name: per_dimension
outputs.description: { directness, rhythm, trust, authenticity, density } — each 0-10

outputs.name: hard_violations
outputs.description: Array of `{ kind, phrase, context }` for every HARD violation found. Empty when text is clean.

outputs.name: soft_violation_count
outputs.description: Count of soft (non-blocking) violations detected. Lower the dimension score but do not block.

outputs.name: by_kind
outputs.description: Histogram `{ <violation_kind>: count }` aggregating both hard + soft violations. Empty when clean.

outputs.name: min_score
outputs.description: Echo of the input min_score (or default 35) — convenient for recipe authors who want to log the gate threshold without re-templating the input.

outputs.name: rubric
outputs.description: Echo of the input rubric (or default `default`) — same convenience as min_score.

text-correct.name: text-correct
text-correct.description: Prescription-pattern AI corrector. A model reads prose against a ruleset and emits a minimal substitution map ([{find, replace, why}]); the component applies it deterministically + a regex backstop. Fact-preserving (only proposed substrings change) and truncation-proof (the model emits a diff, never the full content). Tier-1 per ADR-035 — the ruleset is a parameter, so it corrects against any style guide, not just stop-slop.
text-correct.when_to_use: A second-pass cleanup that FIXES prose instead of only detecting slop. Pair after a draft step and before a verify gate (the 3-beat draft -> text-correct -> stop-slop sequence). Use when re-emitting the full content would risk truncation or fact corruption (large compositions, recipes).
text-correct.when_not_to_use: Need only a pass/fail score with no edits (use `stop-slop`). Need a full creative rewrite rather than surgical fixes (use `inline-llm-pass` with a rewrite prompt). Need human sign-off on the edits (chain with `approval-gate`). The content is deterministic / rule-based with no judgment (use `shell-exec`).

inputs.name: input_text
inputs.description: The prose to correct, inline. Mutually exclusive with `input_text_path`. The component writes it to a temp file so the model reads it the same way it reads a path input.

inputs.name: input_text_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to a file whose contents are corrected in place of `input_text`. Use for large upstream artefacts (a composition / recipe) — pair with the upstream step's `stdout_to_file` + its `outputs.stdout_path`. Mutually exclusive with `input_text`.

inputs.name: ruleset
inputs.description: Path to a markdown ruleset appended to the model as `--append-system-prompt`. Defaults to the stop-slop skill. Swap it for an arabic-voice, brand-voice, or legal-tone ruleset — the corrector itself is style-agnostic. `~` is expanded.

inputs.name: model
inputs.description: Model slug passed verbatim as `--model`. Sonnet is sufficient for a substitution-map pass; Haiku may miss subtle slop.

inputs.name: max_budget_usd
inputs.description: Per-call dollar ceiling (`--max-budget-usd`). The map is small, so this is generous headroom, not a target.

inputs.name: emdash_backstop
inputs.description: When true, a deterministic regex pass converts every em-dash (`—` or ` -- `) to a comma AFTER the model map is applied. Guarantees the hard em-dash ban regardless of what the model missed.

inputs.name: output_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to write the corrected content to. When set, the corrected file is written and `corrected_path` is returned (the full text is NOT echoed to stdout, to bound output size). When omitted, the corrected content is returned inline as `corrected_text`.

inputs.name: claude_binary
inputs.description: Absolute path to the `claude` executable. Tests override to a stub. Production leaves unset — resolves `claude` from PATH.

outputs.name: corrected_path
outputs.description: Path the corrected content was written to. Present only when `output_path` was supplied.

outputs.name: corrected_text
outputs.description: The corrected content inline. Present only when `output_path` was NOT supplied.

outputs.name: substitutions_applied
outputs.description: Count of model-proposed substitutions whose `find` matched and was replaced.

outputs.name: substitutions_missed
outputs.description: Count of proposed substitutions whose `find` did not match the content verbatim (skipped, not guessed).

outputs.name: emdash_fixed
outputs.description: True if the deterministic em-dash backstop changed the content.

outputs.name: cost_usd
outputs.description: Cost of the prescription model call from the CLI envelope. 0 when the call was skipped or stubbed.

outputs.name: exit_code
outputs.description: 0 ok / 2 bad input. The corrector is best-effort — a failed or empty model map does not fail the step; the backstop still runs and the (possibly unchanged) content is written.

vault-write.name: vault-write
vault-write.description: Explicit vault note creation. Routes through POST/PUT /api/vault/notes — no direct-fs fallback. Closes the cafe-ops orphan-project hole.
vault-write.when_to_use: Persist a markdown note to `~/vault/<zone>/...` from a recipe — research output, peer-brief, log entry, ADR draft. Frontmatter is required (zone-aware); body is markdown without the `---` delimiters.
vault-write.when_not_to_use: You need to write outside the vault (use `shell-exec` with `mkdir/echo` — note the soul-cli-guard). You're inside soul-hub app code (call the engine directly via `vault.createNote`). You need a non-markdown asset under the vault (PDF, image — `katib-build` for PDFs).

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Vault-relative path including filename. Must end with .md. Must not contain `..` or start with `/`. The runner is responsible for ensuring this path is under the recipe's declared project zone.

inputs.name: frontmatter
inputs.description: Note frontmatter as a JSON object. Required keys per zone — at minimum `type`, `created`, `tags`. `source_agent` recommended for audit log + rate-limit attribution.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Note body in Markdown. UTF-8. The frontmatter `---` delimiters are added by the API; do NOT include them here.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: `create` POST (refuses overwrite). `replace` PUT (overwrites meta + body). `append` GET-then-PUT (concatenates body to existing).

inputs.name: api_base
inputs.description: Override the Soul Hub base URL. Set this when running against a non-default port.

outputs.name: vault_path
outputs.description: Vault-relative path of the written note (mirrors input `path`).

outputs.name: note_uri
outputs.description: `vault://<path>` URI for downstream references.

outputs.name: bytes_written
outputs.description: Length of the rendered note (frontmatter + body) in bytes.

outputs.name: action
outputs.description: Whether the write created a new note or updated an existing one.

outputs.name: warnings
outputs.description: Non-fatal warnings from the API (e.g., unresolved wikilinks per ADR-047).

inputs.body:
# filter-smoke run {{run_id}}

ADR-006 S2 verification. Filters resolved at recipe-start (defaults)
and at step-time (this body), all against the same ctx.

## Context globals (ADR-006 D2)

- `HOME`: `{{HOME}}`
- `work_dir`: `{{work_dir}}`
- `work_dir | basename`: `{{work_dir | basename}}`
- `work_dir | dirname`: `{{work_dir | dirname}}`
- `date`: `{{date}}`
- `date | date_fmt:DD/MM/YYYY`: `{{date | date_fmt:DD/MM/YYYY}}`

## Default-time filter resolution (ADR-006 S2)

- `inputs.today_short`: `{{inputs.today_short}}` (resolved from default `date_fmt:DD/MM/YYYY`)
- `inputs.work_basename`: `{{inputs.work_basename}}` (resolved from default `work_dir | basename`)

## Step output filter resolution (ADR-006 D3)

- `steps.score.outputs.score`: `{{steps.score.outputs.score}}`
- `steps.score.outputs.score | human_bytes`: `{{steps.score.outputs.score | human_bytes}}` (filter applied to integer — 49 → 49B)

agent-smoke-naseej.name: agent-smoke-naseej
agent-smoke-naseej.description:
First live agent recipe (ADR-005 CP2 keystone). Drives the `inspector`
agent on a tiny verification task with a tight budget override to keep
end-to-end spend under $0.10 per run. Closes falsifier #1 (agent step
end-to-end via the runner).

The task asks inspector to check whether a known file exists in the
working directory and report one of two literal lines. The goal_condition
ends the session as soon as the verification line is written, so the
PTY backend exits gracefully via the goal-loop without burning the
600s default timeout.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `agent:` step type replaced with
`component: agent-dispatch@1.0.0`. Budget fields flattened into inputs.

artifact-smoke-naseej.name: artifact-smoke-naseej
artifact-smoke-naseej.description:
ARTIFACT marker exercise (ADR-005 D7, CP3). Drives inspector to write
a tiny file via Bash + emit the `===ARTIFACT===\n<path>\n===END===`
marker. Verifies the runner's regex (src/lib/naseej/runner.ts line 56)
surfaces `artifact_path` on the step result.

Writes to /tmp/ (not the vault) to keep the file outside the ADR-046
chokepoint — the marker convention is path-agnostic at the runner
layer, this just keeps the smoke deterministic.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `agent:` step type replaced with
`component: agent-dispatch@1.0.0`. Budget fields flattened into inputs.

cancel-smoke-naseej.name: cancel-smoke-naseej
cancel-smoke-naseej.description:
Cancellation smoke (ADR-005 CP3 falsifier #5). Drives inspector with NO
goal_condition and a heavy enumeration task so the dispatcher runs long
enough for an external POST /api/recipes/runs/<id>/cancel to land.

Usage:
  uuid=$(uuidgen | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')
  curl -X POST http://localhost:2400/api/recipes/run \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"recipe\":\"cancel-smoke-naseej\",\"run_id\":\"$uuid\"}" &
  sleep 5
  curl -X POST http://localhost:2400/api/recipes/runs/$uuid/cancel

Expected: the foreground curl returns status 'failed' with the verify
step at agent_status 'cancelled' and exit_code 1.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `agent:` step type replaced with
`component: agent-dispatch@1.0.0`. Budget fields flattened into inputs.

cp4-gate-smoke.name: cp4-gate-smoke
cp4-gate-smoke.description:
CP4 smoke for ADR-023. Exercises the runner pause-intercept hook via
the new `approval-gate@1.0.0` component (component-syntax path, not the
legacy `gate:` step type). Pauses until POST /api/recipes/runs/<id>/respond
fires with a matching `{decision, comment?}` payload.

cp4-human-smoke.name: cp4-human-smoke
cp4-human-smoke.description:
CP4 smoke for ADR-023. Exercises the runner pause-intercept hook via
the new `human-form@1.0.0` component (component-syntax path, not the
legacy `human:` step type). Pauses until POST /api/recipes/runs/<id>/respond
fires with a matching response payload.

filter-smoke-naseej.name: filter-smoke-naseej
filter-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-006 S2 precursor smoke. Verifies the templating engine's new context
globals (HOME, work_dir) + the four filters (basename, dirname,
human_bytes, date_fmt) all interpolate cleanly through input defaults +
step inputs. Composes existing Tier-2 components only — the full
shell-smoke-naseej lands in S3 alongside shell-exec.

gate-smoke-naseej.name: gate-smoke-naseej
gate-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-011 v1 smoke — single gate step. Pauses on POST /run, expects a
POST /respond with {decision, comment?}, returns the decision in
steps.approve.outputs.decision.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `gate:` block replaced with
`component: approval-gate@1.0.0`. All fields moved into inputs.

hello-naseej.name: hello-naseej
hello-naseej.description:
The smallest meaningful Naseej recipe. Composes all 3 v1 components end-to-end:
scores operator text with stop-slop, writes the verdict to the vault, and
notifies via Telegram with the resulting note path. Proves the spine works.

The persist step references `{{now}}` (ISO ms-precision timestamp from the
runner ctx) so repeat runs with the default text remain content-unique and
don't trip the vault chokepoint's content dedup gate.

human-smoke-naseej.name: human-smoke-naseej
human-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-011 v1 smoke — single human step. Pauses on POST /run, expects a
POST /respond with the field payload, returns the response in
steps.ask-name.outputs.response.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `human:` block replaced with
`component: human-form@1.0.0`. All fields moved into inputs.

parallel-smoke.name: parallel-smoke
parallel-smoke.description:
ADR-013 parallel-execution smoke test. Three independent sleep steps in
wave 1, one consolidating step in wave 2. Expected wall-clock with
parallelism: ~3s (longest leaf) + ~1s consolidate ≈ 4s.
Without parallelism the same would take ~3+3+3+1 = 10s.

peer-brief.name: peer-brief
peer-brief.description:
Daily peer-brief PDF render. Naseej port of scripts/peer-brief-render.py
(ADR-007). Five steps compose: signal-trend extraction (shell-exec), recipe
synthesis (peer-brief-synth agent), stop-slop gate (peer-brief-aware scanner
via shell-exec), katib PDF render, Telegram alert.

Faithful to the legacy script for the 5-day shadow window. The stop-slop
scan deliberately invokes the existing peer-brief-aware scanner script
(scripts/peer-brief/stop-slop-scan.py) rather than the generic stop-slop
component, because the scanner extracts prose fields from the katib recipe
YAML and the generic component scans raw text. Swap to the generic component
once peer-brief parity is no longer the bar.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `agent:` step type on synth replaced with
`component: agent-dispatch@1.0.0`. Budget fields flattened into inputs.
Downstream `{{steps.synth.outputs.artifact_path}}` reference preserved.

peer-brief-rebuild.name: peer-brief-rebuild
peer-brief-rebuild.description:
ADR-034 CP3 — the daily peer-brief rebuilt on the internalized Naseej stack:
the peer-brief DOCUMENT TEMPLATE (catalog/documents/peer-brief) + the slot
taxonomy + doc-render. ZERO katib.

Spine: two deterministic context loads (trend SQL + miner-daily split) run in
parallel; a sequential 3-turn claude session chain drafts the JUDGMENT slots
as JSON (so figure labels stay consistent with finding titles — the same
model wrote both); fill-template.py merges static template values +
deterministic data + the JSON drafts into a concrete doc-render composition;
stop-slop scans it as a safety net; doc-render renders the PDF.

The static slots (Why / Method bodies, figure labels, GCC sector columns) and
the deterministic trend chart never hit the model — only genuine analytical
judgment costs tokens. This is the ADR-029 payoff.

Runs as a shadow beside the legacy `peer-brief-daily` cron for a week of
parity (ADR-034 falsifier), then legacy is retired.

per-step-mode-smoke-naseej.name: per-step-mode-smoke-naseej
per-step-mode-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-020 smoke. Two agent steps that explicitly request different dispatch
backends per step. Step 1 pins `mode: production` (claude-pty, goal-loop
active). Step 2 pins `mode: oneshot` (claude-cli-flag, no `/goal` line).
The recipe itself sets no top-level mode — the runner resolves each step's
override independently.

Verification — read the per-run stream:
  - Step 1 logs `[agents/claude-pty] goal-mode active for verify-pty …`
  - Step 2 logs `claude-cli-flag` backend dispatch, no `/goal` line, no PTY
  - Both steps exit 0; total spend <$0.20

Invoke with mode unset (so recipe-wide defaults to 'production') — the
per-step `mode: oneshot` on step 2 must still flip the backend independently.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `agent:` step types replaced with
`component: agent-dispatch@1.0.0`. Per-step `mode:` moved into inputs.
Budget fields flattened into inputs.

quality-check-and-log.name: quality-check-and-log
quality-check-and-log.description:
Score a piece of prose against stop-slop and log the per-dimension breakdown
to the vault under a date-bucketed path. No notification step. Use for
reviewing drafts before sending without triggering Telegram noise.

shell-smoke-naseej.name: shell-smoke-naseej
shell-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-006 S3 keystone smoke (closes F2). Composes every primitive ADR-006
introduced: `file: must_exist=true` input, four templating filters,
HOME + work_dir context globals, and the Tier-1 `shell-exec` component.
No agents, no LLM cost — pure subprocess + vault-write composition.

Flow:
1. Recipe-start resolves `target_file` to a known-good file path and stat-checks it.
2. `shell-exec` runs `wc -l` on the file, redirecting stdout into work_dir.
3. `vault-write` logs the result to inbox/_naseej-runs/ using filters
   (basename, dirname, human_bytes, date_fmt) to verify they all interpolate
   at step time.

text-correct-smoke.name: text-correct-smoke
text-correct-smoke.description: Genericity proof for text-correct (ADR-035 P3 falsifier — >=2 distinct ruleset consumers). The peer-brief-rebuild recipe consumes text-correct with the stop-slop ruleset; this recipe consumes the SAME component with a DIFFERENT, prose-oriented ruleset (British-English spelling) that targets things stop-slop never touches. A passing run (American spellings -> British) proves the corrector follows whatever ruleset it is handed, so it is style-agnostic and not peer-brief-specific. Doubles as a live regression smoke for the component.

timeout-smoke-naseej.name: timeout-smoke-naseej
timeout-smoke-naseej.description:
ADR-011 timeout test — 2s pause, no responder, expect fail.

Re-authored for ADR-023 CP5: `human:` block replaced with
`component: human-form@1.0.0`.
